commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2c182f88741baab74949d3479d51283a4c48f0a4)
commands.review_summary:
Adds Discord REST timeout config, gateway force-drain restart phases, and an opt-in model worker-thread fetch pool wired through provider stream registration and gateway startup.

Reproducibility: no. The PR body describes heartbeat timeouts and event-loop starvation, but it provides no logs, failing test, or live reproduction; source inspection only proves the proposed patch has blocking defects.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor proof plus maintainer design review for provider transport ownership and restart hard-kill policy, not an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(agents): add worker thread pool for event-loop isolation of model calls
target.body:
## Summary

Offloads LLM API `fetch()` calls to a bounded `worker_threads` pool to prevent event-loop starvation on the main gateway thread during long-running streaming model responses.

## Root Cause

Model API calls (Chat Completions, streaming LLM responses) run inline on the main Node.js event loop alongside gateway I/O processing. During long streaming responses, the event loop is blocked, causing:
- Gateway WebSocket heartbeat timeouts
- Delayed message processing
- `fetch-timeout` event-loop starvation warnings

## Solution

**Architecture:**
- Agent context assembly and tool processing remain on the main thread
- Only the actual HTTP `fetch()` for model APIs is routed to workers
- Non-model `fetch()` calls continue on the main thread
- Streaming response bytes pass back through `MessagePort`
- Falls back to main-thread fetch if workers fail

**Worker Pool Design:**
- Bounded pool (configurable `maxWorkers`, default 4)
- Automatic idle worker recycling after inactivity
- Health monitoring and automatic replacement of failed workers
- Graceful shutdown on pool disable
- Global `fetch` override routes matching model API URLs to worker pool

## Config

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "modelWorkerPool": {
      "enabled": true,
      "maxWorkers": 4,
      "timeoutMs": 300000
    }
  }
}
```

## Changes

- **New:** `src/agents/model-worker-pool.ts` — worker pool manager with lifecycle, health monitoring, idle recycling, graceful shutdown. Overrides `globalThis.fetch` to route matching model API calls through worker threads.
- **New:** `src/agents/model-worker.ts` — isolated `fetch()` executor in a `worker_threads` context. Receives requests via `postMessage`, executes `fetch()`, sends base64-encoded responses back.
- `src/agents/provider-stream.ts` — lazy-init worker pool from gateway config on first model call
- `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts` — initialize worker pool after gateway ready
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` config type
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` schema validation
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` labels
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` help text

## Model API URL Detection

The global `fetch` override matches model API URLs via patterns for: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google (Gemini), OpenRouter, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, xAI, Perplexity, Ollama (localhost). All other URLs pass through to the original `fetch`.

## Testing

- [ ] Unit: verify worker creates, processes fetch, returns response via MessagePort
- [ ] Unit: verify pool respects `maxWorkers` limit
- [ ] Unit: verify idle workers are recycled
- [ ] Integration: send streaming model call, verify response arrives through worker
- [ ] Integration: verify non-model fetch calls stay on main thread
- [ ] Integration: kill a worker mid-request, verify fallback to main thread
- [ ] Integration: with pool disabled, verify no global fetch override
target.author: aurorabrain666
